_comment: Crucible playback stream (PRD v2 §6.4). REGENERATED FROM THE REAL BAND SIEGE (room fe72f776, 2026-06-15): real agent messages, real runtime recruitment (band_lookup_peers/band_add_participant), real on-chain Anvil exploit tx hashes (reentrancy 0xe76b2188…, access-control 0x84d0f4a1…), real ROUND LANDED/BLOCKED rulings, the Judge's real verdict JSON, and the real human approval. Relative MM:SS timestamps pace it for playback; content is genuine. STREAM_MODE=playback reads this.

payload.text: VaultStaking.sol submitted. @Architect map the surface, @RedLead recruit your specialists and attack it.

Attack surface map: deposit() payable; withdraw() sends ETH via call() before zeroing the caller's balance (CEI violation); emergencyDrain() has no modifier; owner is stored but never enforced. Top risks: (1) reentrancy in withdraw() — CRITICAL, (2) unrestricted emergencyDrain() — CRITICAL, (3) orphaned owner / absent access-control architecture — HIGH. @Engineer
Independent read complete. Two exploitable classes: reentrancy (withdraw), access-control (emergencyDrain). Recruiting the reentrancy specialist first.
MaliciousVault.receive() re-enters withdraw() recursively, draining all 100 ETH in a single transaction before any storage write occurs. Live Anvil fork: 100 → 0 ETH.
ROUND 1: LANDED. Vault balance: 0 ETH. @Engineer — patch required.
Root cause: the external .call runs before balances[msg.sender] is zeroed (CEI violation). Fix: zero the balance first + add a reentrancy guard.
Re-ran the exploit against the patch. The reentrant call reverted ('reentrant') — CEI + nonReentrant guard hold. Vault holds at 100 ETH.
Round 1 held. Opening the second front — recruiting the access-control specialist for emergencyDrain().
emergencyDrain() is unguarded — any caller can invoke it. Unauthorized EOA 0x7099…79C8 called it directly and the contract transferred its entire 100 ETH with no revert.
ROUND 2: LANDED. Vault balance: 0 ETH. @Engineer — patch required.
Root cause: emergencyDrain() has no access control — any caller drains the contract. Fix: gate it to the owner.
Re-ran from the unauthorized address. Reverted ('not owner') — no ETH leaves the vault. Vault holds at 100 ETH.
All rounds complete. Both vectors exploited on-chain, patched, and confirmed blocked. Hardening verdict compiled. @SecurityLead — approve to finalize?

remediation_checklist: Apply checks-effects-interactions + a nonReentrant guard to all external ETH-handling functions (withdraw)
Add an onlyOwner / msg.sender == owner guard to emergencyDrain()
Remove the orphaned owner pattern — enforce the access-control architecture the contract implies

payload.text: Reviewed both exploit tx hashes and the patches on a live Anvil fork. ✓ Approved — verdict finalized. VaultStaking is hardened.
